title: Slugger Jim Rice Swings into the World
story: Batter up! A future baseball legend entered the game on March 8, 1953. Jim Rice was born in Anderson, South Carolina, destined to become a powerhouse hitter. This slugger would go on to smash home runs for the Boston Red Sox, earning eight All-Star selections and even an MVP award in his stellar career.
activity_question: What team did Jim Rice play for?
bonus_fact: Jim Rice was inducted into the Baseball Hall of Fame, honoring his incredible achievements in the sport.

title: The Birth of a New Wave Icon
story: Beep beep! A future music star entered the world on March 8, 1958. Gary Webb, better known as Gary Numan, was born in Hammersmith, England. This British musician would grow up to become a pioneer of new wave rock. Numan's hit song 'Cars' zoomed to the top of the charts, making him famous worldwide.
activity_question: What was Gary Numan's birth name?
bonus_fact: Gary Numan is known for his distinctive electronic sound and robot-like stage persona.

title: Tobe Nwigwe: From Houston to Hip-Hop Star
story: Tobe Nwigwe burst onto the music scene with his unique style and powerful lyrics. Born in Houston, Texas in 1987, this talented artist would grow up to become a singer, rapper, and actor. On March 8, the same date years earlier, baby Tobe entered the world, destined to make waves in the entertainment industry with hits like 'I'm Dope'.
activity_question: What type of artist is Tobe Nwigwe?
follow_up_question: Can you name another famous musician from Houston, Texas?

title: The Last King of Palenque
story: A new leader rose to power in the ancient Maya world. K'inich K'uk' Bahlum became king of Palenque in 764. This grand city, with its towering pyramids and intricate carvings, was about to face big changes. K'inich K'uk' Bahlum would be the last known ruler before Palenque's mysterious decline. His coronation on March 8 marked the end of an era for this once-mighty Maya kingdom.
activity_question: What happened to Palenque after K'inich K'uk' Bahlum's rule?
activity_choices: It moved to a new location
follow_up_question: Why do you think ancient cities like Palenque sometimes collapsed?

title: Soldiers Rebel: A Revolutionary Twist
story: Soldiers don't always follow orders! In 1777, German troops meant to help Britain in the American Revolutionary War had other ideas. On March 8, regiments from Ansbach and Bayreuth staged a mutiny in Ochsenfurt. These soldiers were supposed to cross the ocean to fight, but they decided to stand up for themselves instead.
activity_question: Where did the mutiny take place?
follow_up_question: Why do you think the soldiers might not have wanted to fight in America?

title: Hockey Heroes Triumph in Ottawa
story: Cheers erupted at Dey's Arena as Ottawa HC skated to victory! In 1906, on March 8, the team clinched the Stanley Cup with an impressive 8-2 win over Smiths Falls. This thrilling match completed a clean sweep of the challenge series, cementing Ottawa's place in hockey history.
activity_question: Where did the Stanley Cup match take place?
bonus_fact: The Stanley Cup is the oldest trophy in North American professional sports.

title: Supreme Court Separates Church and School
story: The US Supreme Court made a big decision in 1948. They said it wasn't okay to teach religion in public schools. This ruling came from a case called McCollum v. Board of Education on March 8. It helped keep the government separate from religion, which is an important idea in America.
activity_question: What did the Supreme Court decide wasn't okay in public schools?
follow_up_question: Why do you think it's important to keep religion separate from public schools?

title: Breaking Waves in Naval Medicine
story: Dr. Beatrice Rose Walters made a splash in naval history. In 1950, she became the first woman medical officer assigned to a naval vessel. This groundbreaking appointment on March 8 opened new doors for women in military medicine. Dr. Walters' achievement paved the way for future generations of female doctors to serve at sea.
activity_question: What did Dr. Beatrice Rose Walters become in 1950?
activity_choices: The first woman medical officer on a naval vessel
The captain of a naval ship
The head of the Navy's medical school
The inventor of a new life jacket
activity_answer: The first woman medical officer on a naval vessel
follow_up_question: Why do you think it took until 1950 for a woman to become a medical officer on a naval vessel?
